reference: What brings you in here today? Um, I just I've had a cough and I've had a sore throat for the past two days and I was just worried that I might have COVID so I thought I'd come in. Did you start having the cough first or the sore throat or both around the same time? It started with a sore throat two nights ago and then it got worse in the morning when I woke up and then I developed the cough, like about a day ago, a day and a half. So this started 2 days ago. Is it constant, are your symptoms constant? Or they come and go? No, they're not constant. They kind of come and go. OK, and do they occur when you're exerting yourself at all or it can happen it? Or do they happen at some point during the day in the morning or night, or just randomly? It's pretty random. Okay. Are you symptom getting any better or worse? Uhm? Maybe a little worse. I kind of feel like I'm also, um, I'm now having like a, a runny nose. A runny nose as well? OK, when did that start? Uhm, maybe like I don't know just now even, like an hour or two ago. OK, uhm have you had any recent exposure to anyone that's been sick recently at all? Uhm? Maybe. I, I work at a hospital. Do you have any other symptoms like. Shortness of breath, chest pain, dizziness, or anything like that? No. No. Okay. And the sore throat are you having trouble or pain while swallowing any food? It's not that bad. Not that bad. It's just irritation in my throat. OK. Any hoarseness in your voice at all? No. Well, OK. Have you traveled at all recently utside of the province? No. Any headaches? No. Any nausea, vomiting? No. Any fevers or chills? Now I know. No OK. Any fatigue or malaise? Maybe a little bit. It's not too bad. OK, any muscle aches at all? Uhm? Yeah, little bit. Okay, and when did that start? Maybe two days ago? OK, and is it just all over your body or in certain locations? It's all over. Have you tried any pain medications? Uhm no, no I haven't. OK. Any changes in your bowel movement at all like Constipation or diarrhea? No. Um, any changes in your appetite? No. OK, have you lost any significant weight recently? Uh, no. OK. Do you have any past medical conditions that you've been diagnosed with? Um, no, I'm pretty healthy. OK. Any allergies to medication? No. Any environmental allergy? No. OK, any previous, so sorry? Are you taking any medications at all right now? Uhm? Um, I'm not, no. Any past hospitalizations or surgeries? Um, no. No. OK, and any family history of any lung or heart condition. Just high blood pressure. My mom has it. OK. Uhm, and currently, are you working? I was working before the symptoms started. OK, and what do you do for work? I work as a custodian at the hospital. OK, and so you've been isolating and have you gotten COVID swab test yet? Uhm no I haven't. OK, so yeah we can do that for you today after a physical, physical examination. Sure. Currently, right now, what's your living situation like? I live alone. OK, and do you live in like an apartment or house? Apartment. OK, and any new exposures at all in your house to dust, new, carpets, animals, anything like that? No. No, OK. And do you smoke? I used to, I don't anymore. I I smoked when I was in high school. Maybe a couple times. And do you smoke marijuana? No. Uh, any alcohol? Um, once once a day, maybe one class of wine at night. Yeah, so that's all the questions I had for you today. Do you have any questions or concerns? No. OK, so yeah, it seems like you've been having these symptoms for the last few days. They seem kind of like viral in nature with your dry cough and runny nose, sore throat. And sometimes with these viral illnesses, it may take some time for the symptoms to go away, especially the cough. So today we will do a COVID swab test for you today, as a lot of the symptoms overlap and you will receive the result in the next one to two days and be contacted by public health for further instructions. As for management, I think you can take over the counter medications like Tylenol cold, drink lots of fluids and stay hydrated. And if those if your symptoms worsen at all, you can contact us again. Sure, thank you. Welcome, take care.
